skill.name: tech-stack-evaluator
skill.description: Evaluate technology stacks, frameworks, cloud providers, costs, security posture, and migration options for engineering decisions.
skill.summary: Compare technology choices with weighted scoring, TCO estimates, security checks, and migration planning.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported a critical heuristic, but review found no command execution, network client usage, secret harvesting, obfuscation, or prompt injection attempt. Most high and medium matches are false positives from markdown examples, security terminology, URL parsing, and technology evaluation vocabulary. Residual risk is low because the skill contains Python scripts, checks one non-secret environment variable, and can write a report to a caller-provided filename.

low_findings.title: Static Critical Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The scanner combined markdown backticks, documentation URLs, and an API key placeholder into a critical heuristic. Review found no subprocess, eval, exec, network client, credential read, or exfiltration behavior in the Python modules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code search found no command execution or network client usage. The cited locations are installation examples, URL input examples, and a non-secret context check.

low_findings.title: Security Terminology Misclassified as Dangerous Keywords
low_findings.description: C2, weak cryptography, and certificate/key alerts are triggered by normal technology evaluation language such as compliance standards, encryption readiness, and scoring terms. No evidence found of malware control logic or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines define compliance feature names and documentation for security scoring. The code does not open certificate files, implement weak crypto, or contact command infrastructure.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Blocks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: External command alerts are caused by markdown fences and example prompts in documentation. These examples are not executable code paths and no Python shell execution APIs were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious markers are markdown code fences and example text. Searches found no subprocess, os.system, popen, eval, or exec calls.

low_findings.title: Non-Secret Environment Context Check
low_findings.description: report_generator.py reads CLAUDE_DESKTOP to choose desktop or CLI report formatting. This is environment access, but it does not read API keys, tokens, passwords, or other secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact environment variable name is visible and is not a credential. The value only changes output formatting behavior.

low_findings.title: User-Directed Report File Write
low_findings.description: The report export helper writes generated markdown to the filename supplied by the caller. This is expected functionality, but callers should avoid untrusted or sensitive output paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is explicit and limited to generated report output. Risk depends on how the caller supplies the filename, so it remains a low operational concern.

content.value_statement: Engineering teams need objective comparisons before choosing frameworks, platforms, or migration paths. This skill turns project context into weighted scores, cost estimates, security checks, and decision-ready recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Compares frameworks, languages, databases, and cloud providers with weighted scoring criteria.
Calculates total cost of ownership using licensing, hosting, training, migration, and scaling inputs.
Assesses security posture and compliance readiness for GDPR, SOC2, HIPAA, and PCI-DSS checklists.
Estimates migration complexity, effort, timeline, risks, and recommended migration approach.
Generates executive summaries and detailed markdown reports for desktop or CLI contexts.
Parses conversational text, JSON-like input, YAML-like input, and URL lists for evaluation requests.
limitations: It does not fetch live GitHub, npm, cloud pricing, or CVE data by itself.
Cost, security, and ecosystem results depend on the data and assumptions provided by the user.
Compliance output is planning guidance, not legal certification or an audit opinion.
Closed-source and internal tools need user-provided metrics before meaningful comparison.

use_cases.title: Choose a Frontend Framework
use_cases.description: Compare React, Vue, Svelte, and Angular for a product team with specific skills, timelines, and maintainability goals.

use_cases.title: Plan a Cloud Provider Decision
use_cases.description: Evaluate AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud for workload fit, cost drivers, team readiness, and long-term platform risk.

use_cases.title: Review Stack Compliance Gaps
use_cases.description: Assess whether a proposed application stack has the security features needed for SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA, or PCI-DSS planning.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare React and Vue for a SaaS dashboard. Focus on developer experience, ecosystem maturity, performance, and hiring risk.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate a Proposed Stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this stack for a real-time collaboration product: Next.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis, WebSockets, and AWS. Include strengths, risks, and a recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Estimate Ownership Cost
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate a five-year TCO comparison for AWS and Azure. Team size is 12 developers, hosting is currently 8000 dollars per month, and usage grows 25 percent yearly.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Migration Decision Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess migration from AngularJS to React for a 75000-line application with 300 components. Include effort, timeline, risks, rollback strategy, SOC2 impact, and decision confidence.

output_examples.input: Compare React and Vue for an enterprise dashboard.
output: Recommendation: React, with medium-high confidence for ecosystem depth and hiring availability.
Vue remains stronger for onboarding speed and simpler state management.
Decision factors include team experience, component library needs, and long-term maintenance risk.

output_examples.input: Estimate TCO for moving a Node.js API from self-hosted servers to AWS.
output: The report separates initial migration cost, yearly hosting, operational support, scaling growth, and hidden costs.
Key cost drivers are infrastructure growth, engineer maintenance time, and migration testing effort.
The recommendation highlights assumptions that should be validated before approval.

output_examples.input: Assess MongoDB and PostgreSQL for a regulated SaaS product.
output: The security section compares access controls, encryption support, audit logging, backup recovery, and compliance readiness.
The migration section identifies schema redesign, data validation, and downtime risks.
The final decision includes confidence level and the main reasons to accept or reject each option.

best_practices: Provide the use case, team skills, timeline, budget constraints, and decision priorities before asking for a recommendation.
Use explicit weights when one factor, such as security or cost, matters more than general platform popularity.
Review assumptions in the TCO, security, and migration sections before using the report for approval.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as the only source for production security or compliance certification.
Do not compare many unrelated technologies without a clear use case and weighted criteria.
Do not treat default cost estimates as final budgets without replacing them with internal numbers.

faq.question: Does this skill collect live data from the internet?
faq.answer: No. The included code parses and evaluates provided data. Users should provide current metrics when accuracy matters.

faq.question: Can it compare more than two technologies?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare several technologies, but decisions are clearer when the list is focused.

faq.question: What inputs improve TCO results?
faq.answer: Provide team size, labor rates, hosting costs, licensing costs, training needs, growth expectations, and migration estimates.

faq.question: Is the security assessment a formal audit?
faq.answer: No. It is a planning assessment for security posture and compliance readiness, not a legal or audit certification.

faq.question: Can it help with migrations?
faq.answer: Yes. It estimates complexity, effort, timeline, risk areas, and a suitable migration approach.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
